response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an accomplished full-stack and systems software engineer with strong throughput and low-latency engineering credentials, but the role demands deep hardware expertise — SoC and IP modeling, SIMD/MIMD architecture, GPU workload analysis, and AI accelerator co-design — none of which appear anywhere in his resume. His AI exposure at Stripe is limited to agentic tooling and MCP frameworks, not deep-learning infrastructure or hardware optimization. The skill gap between his profile and the principal-level hardware architecture requirements is fundamental, not bridgeable by adjacent experience.
strengths: Demonstrated performance engineering in high-throughput, low-latency systems (millions of HTTP requests/second at Sociomantic using async I/O and fiber-based concurrency)
Breadth of systems experience spanning embedded systems, robotic arms, mobile, and distributed infrastructure shows adaptability across computing layers
Recent AI-adjacent work at Stripe on MCP code generators and agentic workflow infrastructure indicates current exposure to the AI ecosystem
concerns: Zero SoC or IP modeling experience — the primary technical deliverable of the role
No GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD, or deep neural network training/inference optimization background, which are all explicitly required or preferred
Entire career is software/application-layer focused; hardware-software co-design and chip-level performance analysis are entirely absent from his history

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP modeling or hardware architecture experience
No DNN/AI accelerator workload optimization background
